text:
Market today:
Rising 10-year Treasury yields are the main headwind, making growth stocks less attractive. This, combined with JPMorgan's warning on AI stocks, sets a cautious tone for your tech-heavy book.

Your holdings:
SOXL (-36.74%, SELL) and SPAL (-55.27%, NEW) are leveraged ETFs with massive drawdowns, amplifying risk on margin. SMH (-7.77%, HOLD) also shows significant loss.

Watch:
Keep a close eye on bond yields and how AI/chip names react to JPM's warning. Volatility is likely.

Bottom line:
Risk management, especially on leveraged positions, is paramount in this environment.
plays: Review SOXL and SPAL: Your current stops are not protective given the deep losses. Re-evaluate your risk tolerance and set realistic, protective stops for these leveraged positions immediately.
Consider trimming QQQ and QQQI: With rising yields and tech warnings, respect the "TRIM" status. If they approach their stops (QQQ 670, QQQI 52), consider reducing exposure.
No new buys today - hold and watch. The macro environment and JPM's warning suggest caution, and scanner setups lack a strong fundamental catalyst for new tech buys now.
